steps.title: Compare the two views
steps.content: Text 1 calls the shared EGR gene an "especially promising discovery" and treats it as exciting progress toward understanding human regeneration. Text 2 reports the same discovery but adds important caution about what the finding does and does not show.

steps.title: Use Text 2's caution
steps.content: Text 2 says the EGR gene likely functions very differently in humans than in panther worms. It also says how this switch operates in humans remains unclear, so Text 2 would not treat the discovery as a clear explanation of human regeneration.

steps.title: Choose the cautious judgment
steps.content: Choice A ignores Text 2's uncertainty about humans, Choice C wrongly treats excitement as unexpected, and Choice D calls Text 1 dismissive even though it is enthusiastic. The remaining choice captures Text 2's caution about Text 1's optimism. The answer is B.
